page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 24 vCPUs mapped to 12 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 90.0 GB of RAM, translating to 3.75 GB of memory per core. It includes a dedicated local storage array consisting of four NVMe SSDs totaling 1343 GB, but does not feature any GPU accelerators.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores placing in the top 25% and top 10% respectively. Single-threaded text processing, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier results. Multi-core performance is generally strong to average, as evidenced by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. The server demonstrates average memory bandwidth across both cached small-block and uncached large-block read/write tests. For specialized workloads, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) support efficient vector and matrix operations, yielding strong performance in small-model LLM text generation and image processing, though large-model text generation remains a bottleneck.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4-standard-24-lssd offers a balanced resource density for compute-heavy workloads that require high-speed local scratch space. The inclusion of 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage eliminates the need for external storage attachments for high-throughput operations. This configuration is highly suitable for tasks such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and small-scale machine learning inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, and 90.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor running at 2.3 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across four drives, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, particularly in text processing and compression tasks, alongside strong multi-core capabilities. The CPU supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX, which enhance vector and matrix calculations. This server is well-suited for compute-intensive applications, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations requiring fast local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd is a compute-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and 1343 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong single-core and compression performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-24-lssd features 24 vCPUs, 90 GB RAM, and 1343 GB NVMe SSD storage for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, high-speed local NVMe storage, and advanced matrix extension support for demanding workloads.
bullet_points: 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor.
90.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core.
1343 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD storage across four drives.
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks.
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via AVX-512 and AMX.
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
